Where is the museum?

key: How do I get to the station?
text: How do I get to the station?

key: Which subway goes to Times Square?
text: Which subway goes to Times Square?

key: How do I get to the airport?
text: How do I get to the airport?
Where is the nearest subway?
Do I need to transfer?

I want to go to Times Square by subway. I find the station and I ask the clerk, which subway goes to Times Square? The red line, she says. A ticket is three dollars. I buy a ticket and I go down to the platform. I do not need to transfer, because the red line is direct. It is about six stops. I watch the schedule on the screen, and soon my train arrives. The subway is fast and easy.

Sometimes I take the bus instead of the subway. I wait at the bus stop and I ask the driver, does this bus go to the park? Yes, he says, it is four stops. I pay and I find a seat. The bus is slower than the subway, but I can see the city. When my stop comes, I press the button and I get off. Taking the bus is a nice way to travel.

key: Excuse me, which subway goes to Times Square?
text: Excuse me, which subway goes to Times Square?

key: The red line goes to Times Square. The station is over there.
text: The red line goes to Times Square. The station is over there.
How much is a ticket?

key: A single ticket is three dollars. Here you are.
text: A single ticket is three dollars. Here you are.

key: Thank you. Where is the platform?
text: Thank you. Where is the platform?

key: Go down the stairs. The platform is on your left.
text: Go down the stairs. The platform is on your left.
Do I need to transfer?

key: No, the red line is direct. No transfer.
text: No, the red line is direct. No transfer.
How many stops is it?

key: It is about six stops. Watch the schedule on the screen.
text: It is about six stops. Watch the schedule on the screen.

key: Great. Where is the bus stop, just in case?
text: Great. Where is the bus stop, just in case?

key: The bus stop is across the street. Have a safe trip!
text: The bus stop is across the street. Have a safe trip!

Elaine wants to go to Times Square. She asks which subway goes there. The clerk says the red line. Elaine buys a ticket. She goes down to the platform. The train arrives and she gets on.
Could you repeat that, please?

key: I am not sure I understand. Could you explain?
text: I am not sure I understand. Could you explain?

key: Could you speak more slowly, please?
text: Could you speak more slowly, please?

key: I am not sure how to say this in English.
text: I am not sure how to say this in English.
Thank you for your patience.
